channel.title: Cadence 步频 — PT Research Signal
channel.description: Daily curated physical therapy & rehab research for clinicians. AI-scored signal from 50+ journals.

item.title: Critically appraised papers: Adding physiotherapy or motivational text messages does not improve pain beyond home exercise alone for degenerative meniscal tear and knee pain [synopsis].
item.description: A critically appraised paper found that adding physiotherapy or motivational text messages did not improve pain beyond home exercise alone for degenerative meniscal tear and knee pain.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Critically appraised paper: Mobile-based pulmonary rehabilitation improved exercise capacity as much as and health status more than centre-based pulmonary rehabilitation for chronic obstructive pulmonary disease [commentary].
item.description: A critically appraised paper found that mobile-based pulmonary rehabilitation improved exercise capacity as much as, and health status more than, center-based pulmonary rehabilitation for chronic obstructive pulmonary disease.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Development of a Core Patient-Reported Outcome (Measures) Set for Pediatric Physical Therapy.
item.description: A study developed a core set of nine patient-reported outcomes (PROs) and corresponding measures (PROMs) for pediatric physical therapy, grouped into three domains (functioning, general well-being, and social/environmental), to standardize assessment in practice, research, and quality assessment.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Frontline agreement in diagnosis and treatment for musculoskeletal dysfunctions. A prospective, randomized, controlled study.
item.description: This RCT found high agreement in diagnosis, treatment, and discharge decisions between physiotherapists (APP role) and resident physicians compared to senior physicians for non-urgent musculoskeletal conditions in the emergency department.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Recent highlights in motor recovery and rehabilitation research after stroke.
item.description: This systematic review identified 118 RCTs, protocols, and systematic reviews with meta-analyses published since 2020, highlighting recent advances in upper and/or lower limb motor training in adults with stroke and providing recommendations for future trials.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Smallest worthwhile effect of anterior cruciate ligament reconstruction compared with non-surgical management: a benefit-harm trade-off study.
item.description: This benefit-harm trade-off study (N=92) estimated the smallest worthwhile effect of anterior cruciate ligament (ACL) reconstruction compared with rehabilitation only for improving knee function and return to sport, and explored associations with participant characteristics.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Role of Platelet-Rich Plasma Injection in Anterior Cruciate Ligament Reconstruction: A Meta-Analysis of Randomized Controlled Trials
item.description: A meta-analysis of randomized controlled trials investigated the role of platelet-rich plasma injection in anterior cruciate ligament reconstruction.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Personalized blood flow restriction training at variable occlusion pressures improves multisystem function in overweight and obese older women - European Journal of Applied Physiology
item.description: This research found that personalized blood flow restriction training at variable occlusion pressures improved multisystem function in overweight and obese older women.
item.author: Sports Medicine

item.title: Improved Flexor Spasticity in Patients with Chronic Stroke Using a Dynamic Wrist-Hand Stretching Device: A Pilot Randomized Controlled Trial.
item.description: A pilot RCT involving 30 chronic stroke patients found that a novel dynamic wrist-hand stretching device improved flexor spasticity and hand function, offering a potential tool for long-term management.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Understanding Adult Hip Dysplasia: Clinical Presentation, Assessment Techniques, and Exercise-Driven Management Strategies
item.description: This review provides an overview of adult hip dysplasia, focusing on its clinical presentation, assessment techniques, and exercise-driven management strategies to improve recognition and intervention.
item.author: JOSPT

item.title: Efficacy and Modulating Factors of Repetitive Transcranial Magnetic Stimulation on Motor Function in Parkinson's Disease: A Meta-Analysis.
item.description: This meta-analysis evaluated the short-term and long-term therapeutic effects of repetitive transcranial magnetic stimulation (rTMS) on motor function in Parkinson's disease, comparing rTMS with sham stimulation and investigating modulating factors.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Establishing weighted criteria for return to play after hamstring injury: a systematic review and methodological quality analysis.
item.description: This systematic review established weighted criteria for return to play after hamstring injury to address high recurrence rates, analyzing studies from PubMed, Sportdiscuss, WOS, and Scielo databases.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Shortwave diathermy for managing knee osteoarthritis: A systematic review with meta-analysis.
item.description: This systematic review with meta-analysis of 17 trials (1,372 participants) found shortwave diathermy (SWD) significantly improved pain (mean -2.13; 95% CI [-2.64, -1.62]; p < .01) and walking ability (mean -0.72; 95% CI [-1.15, -0.29]) in knee osteoarthritis.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Patterns of Outcome Measure Utilization in Stroke Rehabilitation Lower Extremity Trials Conducted in Low-to-Middle versus High-Income Countries.
item.description: This systematic review (N=1,548 RCTs) found that low-to-middle income countries (LMICs) more frequently use generalized impairment and activities of daily living outcome measures relying on patient observation compared to high-income countries (HICs) in post-stroke lower extremity motor rehabilitation trials.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Anti-Compensatory Saccades Changes After Visuo-Vestibular Physical Therapy in People With Acute Unilateral Vestibulopathy: A Prospective Observational Study.
item.description: This prospective observational study (N=92) found that an 8-week structured visuo-vestibular physical therapy (VVPT) program significantly improved anti-compensatory saccades (AcS) assessed by SHIMP in people with acute unilateral vestibulopathy.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Prioritising fall prevention strategies in Parkinson's disease: A multi-method evaluation.
item.description: This multi-method study integrating a structured narrative review, Delphi process (N=15 experts), and Best-Worst Method quantitatively prioritized fall prevention strategies for people with Parkinson's disease.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Associations of neuropathic pain and pain catastrophizing with rehabilitation outcomes in patients with stroke.
item.description: This prospective observational study found that neuropathic pain and pain catastrophizing are common after stroke and are associated with poorer rehabilitation outcomes, including functional status and quality of life.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Multi-Domain Benefits of Exergaming for Sarcopenia Management in Institutionalized Older Adults: Physical, Psychological, and Social Outcomes.
item.description: This pragmatic quasi-experimental study (N=72) found that a 12-week exergaming intervention (Nintendo Switch Ring Fit Adventure) significantly improved physical, psychological, and social outcomes in institutionalized older adults with sarcopenia.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Consensus practice guidelines on interventions for cervical spine (facet) joint pain from a multispecialty international working group
item.description: Consensus practice guidelines on interventions for cervical spine (facet) joint pain were developed by a multispecialty international working group.
item.author: BJSM

item.title: Use of Extracorporeal Shockwave Therapy for the Management of Bone Pathologies: A Systematic Review
item.description: A systematic review evaluated the efficacy and outcomes of extracorporeal shockwave therapy (ESWT) for bone pathologies, including fractures, osteonecrosis/avascular necrosis, bone stress injury, medial tibial stress syndrome, and bone marrow edema syndrome.
item.author: PubMed
